title: scan_block in-memory rollback on save_block_state failure
description: scanner.rs scan_block snapshots tree and witnesses before processing a block, and restores them if save_block_state fails. This ensures the next scan_to_tip call replays the block against a consistent tree rather than double-counting commitments. A contributor might remove the snapshot to save the clone overhead. Preserve: without the rollback, a DB write failure would advance the in-memory commitment tree but not the scan tip, causing the next scan iteration to replay the block with an already-advanced tree — permanently corrupting witness positions.
